rationale: Target home identified as the multi-section hip-roof structure at the base of the cul-de-sac on Kenswick Meadows Ct, occupying approximately pixels (235,555)→(415,885). Roof is a complex hip style with no gable/rake ends visible. Two primary stacking sections were traced: an upper main block (~170×155 px = 36×33 ft ≈ 1,183 sqft) and a lower extension (~140×120 px = 30×25 ft ≈ 754 sqft), minus ~87 sqft for cutouts and alignment, yielding ~1,950 sqft plan-view footprint. Ridge segments counted across 3 runs (~47 px total = ~45 ft). Hip lines estimated at 10 sloped corners averaging ~22 px each (~115 ft). Two to three valley intersections where wings meet (~22 ft). Zero rake lines (fully hipped). Eave perimeter traced around all lower edges (~165 ft). Confidence is moderate-low due to roof complexity, shadow interference on edges, and uncertainty in exact lot/house boundary assignment from satellite view alone.
